name: Soda Bread (Irish Whisky Soda)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine flour, salt and baking soda. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
2. Stir in raisins or currants (if using).
3. In a separate bowl, combine buttermilk, honey and whiskey. Add all at once to dry ingredients; stir just until no dry spots remain.
4. Turn dough out onto lightly floured surface. Knead lightly for 1 minute (too much handling will toughen loaves, while too little will inhibit rising).
5. Divide dough in half and shape each half into an 8-inch round. Place in two greased 8-inch round cake pans.
6. Using a floured knife, cut a cross 1/2 inch deep in each loaf.
7. In a small bowl, combine whiskey and milk. Brush loaves with glaze.
8. Bake in a 350°F (180°C) oven for 35 to 40 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottoms. Remove from pans; let cool on wire racks. Cut into wedges.
